# terrible_netapp_ontap_na_ontap_autosupport (Resource)





<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `host_id` (String) ID of the `terrible_host` to run this task against

### Optional

- `async_seconds` (Number) Run the task asynchronously, timing out after this many seconds. 0 = synchronous (default).
- `changed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered changed (e.g. 'false').
- `delegate_to_id` (String) ID of another terrible_host to delegate execution to.
- `environment` (String) Environment variables set for the task (dict of name→value).
- `failed_when` (String) Jinja2 expression that overrides when the task is considered failed.
- `force` (Boolean) Set the force flag to true to modify some of the AutoSupport configurations that are otherwise blocked when the automatic update feature is enabled. Without this flag set to true, an attempt to disable AutoSupport, modify the transport to SMTP, or disable the AutoSupport OnDemand feature fails if the automatic update feature is enabled.
- `from_address` (String) specify the e-mail address from which the node sends AutoSupport messages.
- `hostname_in_subject` (Boolean) Specify whether the hostname of the node is included in the subject line of the AutoSupport message. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `ignore_errors` (Boolean) When true, a failed task does not raise a Terraform error.
- `is_minimal` (Boolean) Specifies whether the system information is collected in compliant form, to remove private data or in complete form, to enhance diagnostics. Supported only with REST.
- `local_collection_enabled` (Boolean) Specify whether collection of AutoSupport data when the AutoSupport daemon is disabled. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `mail_hosts` (String) List of mail server(s) used to deliver AutoSupport messages via SMTP. Both host names and IP addresses may be used as valid input.
- `max_http_size` (Number) Specify delivery size limit for the HTTP transport protocol (in bytes). Supported only with ZAPI.
- `max_smtp_size` (Number) Specify delivery size limit for the SMTP transport protocol (in bytes). Supported only with ZAPI.
- `nht_data_enabled` (Boolean) Specify whether the disk health data is collected as part of the AutoSupport data. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `node_name` (String) The name of the filer that owns the AutoSupport Configuration. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `noteto` (String) Specifies up to five recipients of short AutoSupport e-mail messages. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `ondemand_enabled` (Boolean) Specify whether the AutoSupport OnDemand Download feature is enabled.
- `partner_addresses` (String) Specifies up to five partner vendor recipients of full AutoSupport e-mail messages.
- `perf_data_enabled` (Boolean) Specify whether the performance data is collected as part of the AutoSupport data. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `poll_interval` (Number) Polling interval in seconds when async_seconds > 0. Defaults to 15.
- `post_url` (String) The URL used to deliver AutoSupport messages via HTTP POST. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `private_data_removed` (Boolean) Specify the removal of customer-supplied data. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `proxy_url` (String) specify an HTTP or HTTPS proxy if the 'transport' parameter is set to HTTP or HTTPS and your organization uses a proxy. If authentication is required, use the format "username:password@host:port".
- `reminder_enabled` (Boolean) Specify whether AutoSupport reminders are enabled or disabled. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `retry_count` (Number) Specify the maximum number of delivery attempts for an AutoSupport message. Supported only with ZAPI.
- `skip_tags` (String) Skip tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `smtp_encryption` (String) The encryption protocol used to deliver AutoSupport messages via SMTP to the configured mail_hosts. Supported only with REST.
- `state` (String) Specifies whether the AutoSupport daemon is to be enabled or disabled. When this setting is absent, delivery of all AutoSupport messages is turned off.
- `support` (Boolean) Specifies whether AutoSupport notification to technical support is enabled.
- `tags` (String) Run only tasks with these Ansible tags (list of strings).
- `timeout` (Number) Override the default execution timeout (seconds). Defaults to 300.
- `to_addresses` (String) Specifies up to five recipients of full AutoSupport e-mail messages.
- `transport` (String) The name of the transport protocol used to deliver AutoSupport messages.
- `triggers` (String) Arbitrary map of values; any change triggers task re-execution
- `validate_digital_certificate` (Boolean) When set to true each node will validate the digital certificates that it receives. Supported only with ZAPI.

### Read-Only

- `changed` (Boolean) Whether the task reported a change
- `id` (String) Unique task resource ID
